messages.content:
Savey said on 01 December 2023

It appears the BBC One Christmas idents this year are focusing on CBBC show KikoRiki, Good considering the characters in that show are anthropomorphic animals, making them awesome for this time of year, I'd assume they will launch tonight in the junction between Doctor Who and Unbreakable, Doctor Who is their highest rating show, so it makes sense to lead into Unbreakable by launching the Christmas idents, Or they could hold it off till tommorow and launch them at Strictly. Whatever works.

Currently still running with the normal dancers idents (when will they be retired, they've been used since 2002)

Emma said on 01 December 2023

Hopefully S4C does something with their new blob mascot, Last year they had TV sets stacked up on top of each other, Then for 10 months they had flickering TV sets, They have finally rebranded and have introduced their first ever mascot: a shape-shifting blob, I hope BBC One rebrand soon, those dancers sure are getting tired, As for the KikoRiki themed Christmas idents, I was told they are launching tonight after Doctor Who, They are being introduced just before Unbreakable.

HouseofFun said on 01 December 2023

Doctor Who ending now, announcement playing "A team of 8 people are pushed to the absolute limit, Unbreakable, our brand new game show is up next, But first, Time to say hello and spend Christmas with the Doctor" I'm not liking this one a whole lot..., They seem to be running a trailer advertising the Doctor Who Christmas special, which revolves around the Doctor having to protect his companions from a man who seeks to find a fob watch (the one The Doctor made himself human with one time), Although just before Doctor Who tonight they had a announcement saying "Christmas is switched on tonight in 1 hour in time for Unbreakable, But first, Is this the end for the Doctor?". Doesn't seem like they are advertising the Christmas pres at all, 

Edit: Christmas pres featuring KikoRiki switched on, First ident shown features Rosa the Pig doing a dramatic ice skate dance (She even uses eyedrops for fake tears) While this is happening we hear dramatic music, The announcer says "Welcome to Christmas on BBC One, Let us entertain you, But now a team of 8 people are being pushed to their absolute limits, Are they Unbreakable?" Not sure why they didn't tell us it was happening untill it did, But still a gorgeous ident, I noticed when the Announcer started talking it sounded as if he was driven to tears by the beauty of Rosa's gorgeous dance. The best thing to come out of this package since the "Lights" christmas idents in 2006, 

Eyes said on 01 December 2023

A beautiful ident, Best thing to come out of the "Rythm and Movement" package since those Planet Earth 3 special idents a few months ago, i feel sad that KikoRiki has not been shown much on CBBC on BBC Two, but I'm glad it's representing the Xmas idents on BBC One (Although I also wish the idents also featured a merman).

Maile said on 01 December 2023

Best Christmas ident in a while, I just love that they have spent it with Rosa and her friends, I hear there is also one featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, I like how they used the usual ident theme (dancing) in that first ident, KikoRiki looks like it's gonna be good this year, but I hope they do more with the style (and maybe finally retire Rythm and Movement idents)

Faramad said on 01 December 2023

News safe ident just on, Basically just a Christmas tree on a snowy background, with a music box Christmas carol playing, on top of that the only thing we see is a sleeping Rosa (We vaguely hear her snoring) Glad they are now using characters in their news safe idents, Announcer said "Now on BBC One, time for the BBC News at 8". Remember the Lights idents the used just before overhauling their promo style in late 2006/early 2007? That was pretty cool, but this is way better. I seem to remember those idents started on a dark street but Christmas lights started turning on before it got to a city center and then people were dancing, It was used again in 2007, And now it seems like the "Project Chameleon" endboard template introduced in July is out the window, at least for now, replaced with a snowy background with a Christmas tree on it. Signs that Rythm and Movement is finally working towards the end.

KikoRiki said on 01 December 2023

We're glad you love our cartoon and the special BBC One idents featuring our characters, 

However, Rosa will be the star, (We will have idents revolving around Chiko, Krash and the others), As to what you said, BBC One are looking into introducing the Circle idents already in use in other countries, so don't expect to see Ballet, Capoeria or Festival/Rave back in the new year. Get used to Kites, Capes, Hippos and Bikes as that's what might be coming after our Christmas campaign, We don't know for certain, but the Dancers might not be coming back after Christmas

WhovianForever: Did everyone catch the Doctor Who Christmas special announcement during the news? I’m so hyped! Can’t wait to see how the Doctor deals with the fob watch saga this holiday season!

LemonyFan: And how about they’re showing the 2004 ‘A Series of Unfortunate Events’ movie!! That’s one of my holiday favorites; the nostalgia is real. 😍🍿

ChristmasCarol: Truly is shaping up to be a wonderful holiday schedule on BBC One. Between Doctor Who and the classic movies, I’m going to be glued to my screen with all the mulled wine and mince pies!

KikoFanatic: Plus, the KikoRiki Christmas idents have been adorable so far! Rosa’s ice skate dance nearly brought tears to my eyes just like the announcer. 🐷⛸ Christmas is definitely switched on!

RetroTV: I’m just here wondering if the new ‘Circle’ idents KikoRiki hinted at is a sign we’re finally saying goodbye to the “Rythm and Movement” package for good. After 20+ years, it’s about time for a major refresh!

NostalgiaNerd: @RetroTV I’m with you on that. The Lights idents back in '06/'07 were beautiful. It’s cool to see they’re still willing to shake things up visually with the news safe ident this year. Sleepy Rosa might just be my new favorite. 😴🎄

SeriesBinger: Doctor Who special + A Series of Unfortunate Events + KikoRiki idents = Best Christmas TV lineup or what? Also, hope BBC One throws in some classic Christmas episodes of our favorite shows. Maybe some ‘Vicar of Dibley’ or ‘Only Fools and Horses’?

CircleCurious: Anyone heard more about these new Circle idents? I love change, and KikoRiki characters have been great, but I’m already excited for what’s coming next. Bring on the new era!

KikoLover: Just saw a new Krash and Chiko ident! They’re having a blast playing in the snow! ❄️🐇 So cute watching Krash throw snowballs while Chiko dodges around. Really captures the winter fun vibes.

SciFiSavvy: Speaking of new idents, did anyone else notice the Dokko the Moose building a laser device ident? It aired right before ‘Doctor Who’ and set the PERFECT tone for sci-fi. Dokko’s always been the tech genius of the group. 🦌🔧

HedgehogAdmirer: @KikoLover Chiko is always adorable, but seeing him in the snow was next level! BBC One knows how to do Christmas idents right. Plus, the animation on these characters in the idents is super impressive this year.

WhovianGuru: @SciFiSavvy Loved the Dokko ident! It’s like a mini episode of ‘Doctor Who’ in itself. Brilliant tie-in to the show’s theme! Makes waiting for the Christmas special even more exciting.

BakeMaster: But guys, let’s talk about the Olga the Owl baking ident. The detail on that gingerbread house she was making! I can almost smell the cookies through the screen. 🦉🍪 It’s the cozy ident I never knew I needed in my life.

HolidayWatcher: Right?! The Olga ident feels like being wrapped in a warm blanket with a hot chocolate in hand. It’s chef’s kiss perfection. BBC One’s going all out with these KikoRiki characters for Christmas.

DoctorFan: Dokko’s ident was like a mini teaser for what’s coming with ‘Doctor Who’. Immersive and full of that tech mystery vibe. I appreciate that BBC One creates these unique intros for thematic continuity.

AnimationAppreciator: Major kudos to the animation team for the KikoRiki idents. As someone who dabbles in animation, the quality is sensational. It’s like a little holiday gift before each show starts!

ChristmasCheer: Can’t get enough of the snow play with Krash and Chiko. It’s absolutely delightful and spreads so much joy. These little moments provided by the idents add a special touch to the holiday season.

SciCompanion: Dokko and his inventions always remind me that anything’s possible with a little imagination. Such an inspiring ident to lead into ‘Doctor Who’. Well played, BBC One! Can’t wait to see what else is in store, I'm gonna miss these idents when new year comes around! 😢

WelcomeToSciFi: The Dokko centric ident really sets the scene for what's to come on ‘Doctor Who’ this Christmas. One of the best things about the KikoRiki set, Far better than the usual dancers, Come to think of it, they have been using the dancers since 2002, so now is a good time for a revamp, I guess 😉

Olgo5: Just saw the Olga ident and damn, Olga really is a talented chef, They always seem to show it before cooking shows, Which ident normally leads into Eastenders, It seems to vary between Krash and Chiko in the snow, Olga cooking and Rosa ice skating. Not sure how this is consistent, But that Rosa one brings  tears to my eyes with her graceful skating, Not sure why the Dokko one only plays before Doctor Who, Maybe because it fits it well

Prolouge:

User1: So, what’s everyone thinking BBC One’s going to pull out of the hat for this year’s Christmas idents? Last year was decent, but I’m hoping for something with a bit more pizzazz.

WhovianForever: I’m crossing my fingers for something Doctor Who-themed! With David Tennant back in the show earlier this year, it would be perfect timing to capitalize on that for the festive season. Can you imagine an ident with the TARDIS all decked out in Christmas lights?

HolidayJoy: That would be cool for us Whovians, for sure, but BBC One usually goes for something that appeals to everyone. Doctor Who is big, but not universally loved by every single viewer. Plus, they tend to lean towards more generic festive symbols.

SeriesBinger: True, they’ll probably aim for something more inclusive. Like a cozy winter scene or playful animation. Maybe bring back some classic characters or even partner with a popular children’s show this time around for wider appeal?

RetroTV: I’ve got a hunch they’ll go with animation this year. It has that universal charm, right? Plus, it would work perfectly with both the kids’ content and the family-friendly vibe they love during Christmas.

User1: That’s an interesting angle. They could get quite creative with animation. Plus, they’re probably due for a change since the “Rythm and Movement” dancers have been around since 2002.

KikoFanatic: What if they teamed up with a show like KikoRiki? The characters are fun, visually engaging, and it’s something that would stand out. Plus, they could do loads with those quirky little animals.

LemonyFan: KikoRiki would be such a cute choice! It could be a hit with children and adults alike if done right. And it’s refreshingly different from usual idents, without relying on something niche like a specific Doctor Who theme.

WhovianForever: Okay, I see your point. Much as I’d love Doctor Who idents, something like KikoRiki could offer that ‘Christmas with the family’ vibe they probably want. I just hope they add a little extra magic this year, you know?

CircleCurious: Whatever they do, I hope it’s something new. We’ve had the “Rythm and Movement” package idents for such a long time. It’s high time for something fresh and exciting to take us into the new year! Write a blog post about the ident which introduced news and other serious programmes (e.g The Andrew Marr Show)

### A Cozy, Serene Touch to News and Serious Programmes: BBC One's News Safe Ident

As the festive season approaches, television channels traditionally seek ways to sprinkle a bit of Christmas spirit into their broadcasting schedule. This year, BBC One has taken a heartwarming, serene approach to introduce news and other serious programmes like The Andrew Marr Show, diverging from the usual upbeat or vibrant themes of the season. Central to this approach is their latest "News Safe" Christmas ident, a beautifully crafted piece that blends the festive spirit with a tranquil mood suited for more serious programming.

At the heart of the ident is Rosa, a character from the internationally beloved children’s show, KikoRiki, known for its anthropomorphic animal characters, engaging storylines, and educational messages. Rosa takes center stage in this new ident, which features a simplistic yet evocative setting of a Christmas tree on a snowy background. What sets this apart is the tranquil ambiance created through the visuals and the accompanying music box rendition of a Christmas carol, which lends an almost ethereal quality to the moment.

In the ident, viewers are greeted with the sight of a gently snoring Rosa, asleep under the soft glow of the Christmas tree lights, amidst gently falling snow. This tableau creates a stark contrast to the usual high energy or dynamic visuals expected during the holiday season, especially in idents launching programs of a more solemn nature.

BBC One's choice to incorporate a character from a children's show into the ident for news and serious programmes is both bold and endearing. It serves as a gentle reminder of the warmth and hope that the festive season brings, even when addressing the more serious aspects of everyday life. The sleeping Rosa, with her peaceful demeanor, beautifully encapsulates the season's essence of peace and goodwill.

This "News Safe" Christmas ident marks a departure from BBC One's traditional approach to festive broadcasting. By including a subtle nod to the joy and innocence found in children's programming, the ident brings a unique form of storytelling to the fore. It's a narrative that acknowledges the complexity of the world while offering a moment of calm, comforting viewers with the assurance that, despite the world's hustle and bustle, there remains a place for tranquility and gentle cheer.

Moreover, this ident reflects a broader trend in broadcast design, where channels are increasingly seeking innovative ways to weave narratives into even the shortest of broadcasts. It's a testament to BBC One's commitment to not only entertain but to bring a sense of community and shared experience to its audience, particularly during a season as reflective as Christmas.

In a world that often feels like it's moving too fast, this news-safe Christmas ident from BBC One is a welcome pause, a moment of serenity amidst the noise. It's a reminder that, even in the rush of current events and the demands of staying informed, there's value in taking a moment to appreciate the simple, quiet beauty of the holiday season. This year, BBC One has indeed given viewers a beautiful, albeit brief, escape into a world of calm and peace, setting a contemplative tone for the news and serious programmes that follow.
Write a forum convo wondering what ident is used into sports

**Thread Title: Which Ident Leads Into Sports on BBC One?**

**FootyFan88**: Hey everyone! I’ve been noticing the variety of idents BBC One has been rolling out for different programmes. Got me wondering, what’s the specific ident used to lead into sports broadcasts? 🤔⚽️

**CricketLover101**: Good question, @FootyFan88! Last I checked, they often use something that vibes with energy and motion to match the excitement of live sports. But, haven’t pinned down if there’s a specific one for all sports. Anyone else noticed?

**RugbyRalph**: Yeah, I’ve noticed that too, @CricketLover101. It varies, but it’s always something that gets the adrenaline pumping. Last weekend before the rugby match, they used an ident that had this dynamic motion theme, felt almost like you're moving through a tunnel rapidly, echoing the anticipation of a match. 

**SkateboardSkye**: It’s pretty cool how they set the mood before the game even starts. Wish they'd use something extreme sports themed once in a while. Imagine an ident with BMX or skateboard tricks for the lead into extreme sports events. 🛹🚴

**TennisAce**: Does anyone remember the ident with the bouncing balls? I thought that was clever, especially when it led into Wimbledon last year. Gave it a nice touch, aligning with the tennis vibes.

**GolfGuru**: Personally, I miss when the idents were simple. Now it feels like each one’s trying to outdo the last in terms of creativity and animation. But I get it, it’s about capturing the essence of the upcoming programme. 

**MarathonMike**: True, @GolfGuru. Though, it does make each broadcast feel special. For the Marathon, the ident that played had this fluid, forward motion vibe – made you feel like you were already on the run. Adds to the whole experience if you ask me.

**SwimmerSally**: Has anyone seen the ident specifically made for swimming? I heard rumors about one with a water theme – creating ripples or something to that effect. Would love to see how they creatively transition to aquatic sports. 🏊‍♀️

**FootyFan88**: Some great points here! Clearly, BBC One puts a lot of thought into how they transition viewers into the sports mindset. It would be interesting to get a behind-the-scenes look at how they choose and create these idents. Maybe they consider the specific sport’s dynamics to design an ident that harmonizes with the energy and anticipation of the upcoming event.

**SpeedSkater**: Absolutely, @FootyFan88. It’s all about building that anticipation and excitement. I guess the essence is to get viewers in the right frame of mind and pumped for the sport they’re about to watch. Each sport has its own rhythm and pace, and a well-chosen ident can mirror that brilliantly.

**CricketLover101**: Well said, everyone! It’s fascinating how a brief ident can influence the viewer’s mood and anticipation for the sports broadcast. Hats off to the creatives behind the scenes. Would love to keep this convo going if anyone catches a unique sports lead-in!

**AdminNote**: **Reminder to all members** - If you manage to capture or describe any unique sports idents, feel free to share here! Let’s keep the discussion respectful and focused on the art of broadcast presentation. Happy watching! 📺🎉

---

This forum conversation highlights the community's curiosity and appreciation for the creativity behind BBC One's idents, particularly those leading into sports broadcasts. Each contribution underscores a recognition of the thought process involved in aligning the visual introduction with the sport's essence, enhancing the overall viewing experience.
Write a convo during Christmas 2023 asking which out of the Kikoriki Christmas set is used before sports

**Thread Title: Which Kikoriki Christmas Ident for Sports Broadcasts 2023?**

**SoccerSanta23**: Hey folks! 🎄 With all the festive Kikoriki idents on BBC One this December, I’m trying to figure out – which one are they using to lead into sports broadcasts? Caught a couple of matches but missed the intros. Anyone in the know?

**HolidayHooper**: Just tuned into a basketball game yesterday and they played the one with Krash and Chiko throwing snowballs! 🏀❄️ Seemed fitting, considering the fast pace and playful nature of both the ident and the sport.

**XmasSkier**: Ah, that makes sense! Was wondering if they had a special Kikoriki ident for winter sports. Would love to see Dokko the Moose getting into some snowboarding action before the skiing competitions start. 🎿

**FestiveFootyFan**: During the football (soccer for our American friends 😉) match, they actually used the ident where Rosa the Pig is ice skating. I guess the grace and agility connect well with the footballers’ skills on the pitch. ⚽️⛸

**JingleJogger**: Has anyone seen a sports-specific Kikoriki ident though? Like, one that was clearly made with sports in mind? Or are they cleverly using the existing Christmas set to match the vibe of each sport?

**SnowballSnooker**: Personally, I caught the snooker championship, and they aired the one with Chiko building a snowman. Maybe because snooker requires patience and a bit of a slower pace, akin to carefully crafting a snowman? 🎱☃️

**RugbyReindeer**: Interesting! For the rugby highlights, it was the snowball fight ident again. Seems like the choice leans towards dynamic and playful scenes, reflecting the energetic atmosphere of sports events.

**SkatingSpectator**: Totally caught the Rosa ice skating ident before figure skating. Her elegant moves on the ice were the perfect lead-in to the athletes’ performances. It’s pretty smart how they match the idents’ themes with the sports’ essence.

**HockeyHolly**: Makes me wonder if they consider audience expectations or if they’re going for a universal appeal with each ident. Either way, the Kikoriki idents add a nice festive layer to the sports viewing experience!

**FestiveFootyFan**: Definitely gives each broadcast a bit of extra holiday cheer! I’m just enjoying the creativity. Though, now I’m curious to see if they’d introduce any new idents specifically tailored to big sporting events in the future.

**JingleJogger**: Agreed! It’s fun trying to guess which Kikoriki ident will play next. Adds an extra layer of anticipation to the sporting events. Here’s to hoping for more themed idents tailored to the sports they precede!

**AdminFrosty**: **Quick Reminder**: While enjoying the festive season, let’s keep sharing our ident sightings and thoughts on how well they match up with the sports broadcasts! Merry watching, everyone! 📺🎅

---

This conversation showcases the curiosity and engagement of viewers with the creative decisions behind the selection of Christmas idents, specifically those from the Kikoriki series, before sports broadcasts. Each member brings their observations and preferences to the table, reflecting a community that appreciates the thoughtful pairing of festive visuals with the athletic prowess showcased in various sports.
Write a follow up before the showing of ASOUE and a user says the ident used, and the announcement over it

**Thread Title: Anticipation for A Series of Unfortunate Events (ASOUE) on BBC One Tonight!**

**CountOlafFan**: Can't wait for the showing of A Series of Unfortunate Events! Does anyone know which Kikoriki Christmas ident they’re going to use before it starts? And what's the announcement going to be like? 📚🍿

**LemonySnicketLover**: Just caught a preview! They used the ident with Krash the Rabbit excitedly opening his presents. It had a fun, anticipatory vibe that matches well with the excitement (and unfortunate events) that the Baudelaire orphans experience. 🐰🎁

**VFDVolunteer**: Really? That’s fitting! Do you remember what the announcement over it was like?

**LemonySnicketLover**: Yes! The announcer mentioned, "Now on BBC One, join us for a tale of woe and wonder as the Baudelaire orphans navigate through A Series of Unfortunate Events. Will fortune ever smile upon them? Find out next." It set the stage perfectly for the atmosphere of the series. 

**SunnySideUp**: That’s so clever! The anticipation of Krash with his presents paralleling our anticipation for the show. And the announcement sounds like it catches the mood just right. Can’t wait!

**TheInventor**: Love how they use these Kikoriki idents to add an extra layer of charm before the show. It’s all about setting the mood, and it sounds like they’ve nailed it for ASOUE tonight!

**BookwormBaudelaire**: Has anyone else noticed the subtlety in how the announcement wording echoes Lemony Snicket’s own narrative style? It’s a delightful nod to the fans. Can't wait to see how BBC One brings our beloved books to life again tonight.

**QuagmireTriplets**: Absolutely, @BookwormBaudelaire! The announcement’s phrasing and the playful yet mysterious ident choice seem intentionally designed to immerse viewers right from the start. It heightens the anticipation!

**ASOUEAdmirer**: These details make such a difference in enjoying our favorite shows and movies. Kudos to BBC One for creatively bridging the idents and announcements with the thematic elements of ASOUE. 🎩

**CountOlafFan**: Thanks for the update, @LemonySnicketLover! This makes me even more excited. It’s almost as if BBC One is casting us in our own series of fortunate (viewing) events ahead of the main show. 🎭

**AdminAnnouncement**: **Friendly Reminder**: Grab your popcorn and a comfy seat because the world of ASOUE awaits you tonight on BBC One. Let's enjoy the show together, and feel free to share your thoughts and favorite moments here after the airing!

---

This fictional forum conversation highlights the excitement and speculation within a community of fans eager for the broadcast of A Series of Unfortunate Events on BBC One, including the perfect matching of a Kikoriki Christmas ident to precede the show. The attention to detail in the announcement's wording enhances the viewing experience, demonstrating the power of thoughtfully crafted introductions to set the mood for beloved series and films.
Write a convo on January 1 at 3am about the last ident used, and the first ident used after the campaign ended at 6am

**Thread Title: The End of the Kikoriki Christmas Campaign and the First Ident of the New Year!**

**MidnightWatcher**: Did anyone else stay up to catch the last Kikoriki Christmas ident at 3am? It felt a bit surreal watching it for the last time. Wonder what BBC One has lined up for us after the campaign officially ends. 

**EarlyBird**: Yeah, the last airing of Rosa the Pig doing her dramatic ice skate dance right at 3am was a bittersweet moment. It’s been such a charming campaign. Can’t believe we’re moving away from the Kikoriki gang already.

**NewYearNewIdent**: Just caught the first post-campaign ident at 6am! It was a refreshing change—no specific show tie-in, just a beautifully crisp and serene scene of a sunrise over a British landscape, signaling the dawn of the new year. The announcer’s message was all about new beginnings and looking forward to 2024. Quite fitting.

**IdentAficionado**: That sunrise ident sounds like the perfect transition into the new year. BBC One always knows how to set the mood. Did anyone manage to record it? I’d love to see it if I can; slept right through the changeover. 

**DawnWatcher**: @IdentAficionado, I got it! The gentle transition from night to day was captured beautifully, making it a seamless move away from the festive season. The announcer's optimistic tone about 2024 really complemented the visual. I'll see if I can share a clip.

**SeasonalViewer**: It’s always a bit nostalgic saying goodbye to the Christmas idents, especially one as unique as the Kikoriki campaign. But that sunrise ident sounds like a breath of fresh air—new beginnings indeed.

**EarlyBird**: True, @SeasonalViewer. I think shifting from the whimsical Kikoriki to the serene, hopeful imagery of a new dawn encapsulates the essence of transition into the new year perfectly. Kudos to BBC One for that thoughtful portrayal.

**Timekeeper**: It's interesting to consider the symbolism behind these choices. Kikoriki represented a season of joy, community, and perhaps a bit of escapism, while the sunrise marks a return to reality but with a hopeful outlook. It’s like visual storytelling done right.

**NewYearNewIdent**: Exactly, @Timekeeper. It's that blend of ending something special but looking forward to the promise of what’s next. Makes you appreciate the careful thought that goes into programming and presentation, even in something as brief as an ident.

**IdentEnthusiast**: I'm just excited to see what BBC One has planned for the rest of the year. If the sunrise ident is anything to go by, we're in for some visually stunning and emotionally resonant content.

**MidnightWatcher**: Agreed. If these transitions and choices are signs of what’s to come, we’re in for a treat. Here’s to a year of meaningful, beautiful broadcast moments. Happy New Year, everyone! 🌅🥂

**AdminReminder**: **Just a note for everyone**: Let's keep sharing our thoughts and findings on the broadcast journey of BBC One throughout the year. Here's to more engaging conversations and a community that appreciates the art behind broadcasting. Cheers to 2024! 🎉
Write a follow up where it's back to the usual idents at 9am, the circles are coming later in the year, probably late summer

**Thread Title: Return to the Usual Idents and Anticipation for the Circle Idents**

**MorningGlow**: At 9am, BBC One switched back to the well-known ‘usual idents’—the dancers are back. After the stunning, albeit temporary, departure to the Kikoriki Christmas special and the serene sunrise ident, it feels like greeting an old friend. Thoughts on returning to the norm before the big Circle rollout?

**VisualVoyager**: Honestly, it's comforting but also highlights how ready I am for a major refresh. The sunrise ident was a tantalizing teaser of what BBC One can do visually. Knowing the Circles are coming, presumably late summer, just ramps up the anticipation for me.

**DancerDefender**: There's something timeless about the ‘usual idents’ that I love, but I acknowledge it’s time for change. The Circles teaser has me excited. It’s like waiting for a new chapter to start in BBC One’s visual storytelling.

**SummerSeeker**: Late summer feels so far away for the Circle idents! But anticipation is part of the fun. It gives us something to look forward to, and I’m eager to see how the new design shakes up the channel’s aesthetic.

**ChangeChampion**: Agreed, @SummerSeeker. BBC One’s evolution in its idents is a brilliant way to keep the channel dynamic and reflective of contemporary design trends. Plus, it keeps conversations like ours lively and interesting!

**IdentInsider**: From what I've seen teased about the Circle idents, we're in for a real treat. They represent a modern, more universal approach to branding, which can only be good for the channel’s identity amidst the rapidly changing media landscape.

**NostalgiaNerd**: As much as I’m looking forward to the Circles, there’s a part of me that will miss the old idents. They’ve been a backdrop to so many significant moments over the years. Hopefully, the Circles can live up to that legacy.

**PreviewPundit**: Does anyone know if there are going to be special variants of the Circle idents for different seasons or events? That’s one thing I’ve always appreciated about the current and past idents—their adaptability.

**EagerEye**: Great question, @PreviewPundit. It would be a smart move to maintain that level of flexibility with the Circle idents. It keeps the brand fresh and allows for some creative freedom within the established design framework.

**CircleCurator**: While we wait for the new idents, it's an opportune time to appreciate the craft that goes into these mini art pieces. They're not just fillers; they’re a significant part of the viewing experience, setting the tone and embodying the channel's identity.

**AnticipationArtist**: Let’s enjoy the journey back through the ‘usual idents’ as we anticipate the new era with the Circles. It’s going to be a summer of change, and I can’t wait to discuss each new ident as it airs.

**MorningGlow**: Love this enthusiasm and reflection, everyone! It shows how much these small pieces of broadcast content mean to us as viewers. Here's to enjoying the present while looking forward to what the summer will bring with the Circle idents. 🌞🔄

**AdminAnnouncement**: **Friendly Reminder**: Let's keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Speculations, hopes, and memories are all welcome as we celebrate BBC One’s broadcast history and look forward to its future. Stay tuned for updates on the Circle idents, and happy viewing, everyone!
Write a forum convo about the Xmas idents used in 2024

**Thread Title: Reflecting on BBC One's Xmas Idents for 2024**

**FestiveWatcher**: Can’t believe how unique the BBC One Xmas idents have been this season! They really went all out for 2024. Did anyone have a favorite? Mine had to be the one with the animated snow globe scenes. Felt like a cozy winter night in front of the TV.

**YuleTideYouTuber**: Yes! The snow globe ident was pure magic. Did anyone catch the one with the stop-motion reindeer playing football? It was such a playful twist and a nice nod to the World Cup happening around the same time.

**CarolSinger**: I loved the reindeer football! But my heart was stolen by the choir of animated penguins. It was not just visually stunning, but the harmonies they “sang” really got me into the holiday spirit. 

**EggnogEnthusiast**: Both are great, but did anyone else appreciate the simplicity and beauty of the Northern Lights ident? It was mesmerizing to watch and made staying up for the late-night Christmas specials even more worth it.

**ChristmasCritic**: They've certainly stepped up their game. I'm all for the innovation BBC One's showcased, especially after the introduction of the Circle idents earlier. The variety this Christmas feels like a fresh extension of that creativity.

**PuddingPrepper**: Absolutely, @ChristmasCritic. It’s like each ident added its own little story to the festive season. And the way they transitioned seamlessly into the programs added such a charming touch to the whole viewing experience.

**WinterWonderFan**: That collaboration paid off! Each ident brought its own warmth and wonder. I also appreciate how inclusive they were, reflecting different holiday traditions and celebrations.

**GiftWrapperGuru**: For sure, @SleighRider! It’s been a while since I felt that commercial breaks didn’t interrupt the festive mood but rather enhanced it. Kudos to BBC One for keeping us engaged and entertained in every aspect of the broadcast.

**NoelNostalgic**: Brings back memories of the “Circle” idents' debut earlier this year. There was a lot of curiosity about how BBC One would blend their new direction with the Christmas season. Safe to say, they’ve exceeded expectations with these idents!

**RibbonTwirler**: So true, @NoelNostalgic. I was a bit skeptical at first but seeing how beautifully everything came together for the Christmas season has been a delight. Here’s hoping they keep this level of creativity going for future holidays!

Write a page about the 2023 Kikoriki ones aying the programs they were introduced in the break between, and say this is the last Christmas ident package during the R&M era

### The 2023 Kikoriki Christmas Idents: A Festive Farewell to the Rhythm & Movement Era

The 2023 festive season marked a significant moment in BBC One's broadcasting history, presenting what would be the last Christmas ident package crafted under the long-standing Rhythm & Movement (R&M) era. This year, the channel embraced the whimsical charm of Kikoriki, a beloved animated series known for its colorful characters and heartwarming narratives, to bid a fond farewell to an iconic era of visual storytelling on BBC One.

### The Final Flourish of Rhythm & Movement

For over two decades, the R&M idents have been a familiar visual motif on BBC One, accompanying viewers through countless seasons and celebrations. Yet, as the media landscape evolves, so too must the faces and themes that grace our screens. In choosing the vibrant world of Kikoriki for its final Christmas ident package within the R&M framework, BBC One infused its airwaves with a blend of nostalgia and novelty — a fitting tribute to a beloved era drawing to a close.

### Kikoriki: Bringing Joy and Whimsy to the Holidays

The decision to feature Kikoriki characters in the 2023 Christmas idents was met with enthusiasm and affection from viewers of all ages. Each ident in the series introduced programs with an array of whimsical scenarios featuring fan-favorite characters like Rosa the Pig, Krash the Rabbit, and Chiko the Hedgehog, each bringing their unique charm to the festive season.

**1. Doctor Who Christmas Special:** The launch of the Kikoriki Christmas idents was strategically placed in the junction between "Doctor Who" and "Unbreakable," tapping into the high viewership and excitement surrounding the Time Lord’s latest adventure. The first ident showcased Rosa the Pig performing a dramatic ice skate dance, symbolizing the elegance and mystery synonymous with "Doctor Who."

**2. Unbreakable:** Following the intergalactic intrigue of "Doctor Who," "Unbreakable" debuted with the Kikoriki gang pushing the boundaries of physical and emotional endurance. The dramatic flair of Rosa's ice skating resonated with the themes of perseverance and strength featured in this brand-new game show.

**3. Strictly Come Dancing Christmas Special:** A highlight of the festive schedule, "Strictly Come Dancing" was introduced by an ident featuring Krash the Rabbit opening his presents, mirroring the anticipation and delight experienced by viewers tuning in to see their favorite stars take to the dance floor.

**4. BBC News at Ten:** Even the most serious program on the Christmas schedule, "BBC News at Ten," was touched by the Kikoriki charm. A "news-safe" ident, featuring a serene scene of a sleeping Rosa near a softly lit Christmas tree, provided a gentle transition into the news broadcast, blending the spirit of the season with the gravity of the day's events.

### A Look to the Future

As BBC One bids farewell to the R&M era with the Kikoriki Christmas idents, anticipation builds for the introduction of the Circle idents slated for launch in the following year. This transition signifies a fresh visual direction for the channel, promising innovative designs that reflect the evolving tastes and sensibilities of its audience while maintaining the warmth and inclusivity characteristic of BBC One’s broadcasts.

The 2023 Kikoriki Christmas idents, thus, stand as a vivid and joyful coda to a memorable chapter in BBC One’s history, celebrating the past while paving the way for new traditions and visual narratives to emerge.
